indication: The treatment of unresectable or metastatic melanoma in patients aged 12 years or more where the following criteria are met: the patient has unresectable stage III or stage IV histologically confirmed melanoma; the patient is aged 12 years or older; the patient has not received previous treatment for this indication with anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-PD-L2, or anti-CTLA-4 antibodies; the patient is of ECOG performance status 0 or 1 (or Lansky score ≥80% if aged 12-17 years); the patient has no symptomatic brain metastases or leptomeningeal metastases currently requiring steroids.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Nivolumab 480mg combined with Relatlimab 160mg as fixed-dose combination (Opdualag). Flat dose — doses are delayed, not reduced, for toxicity. Use in-line or add-on sterile, non-pyrogenic, low protein binding filter (pore size 0.2–1.2 µm). Compatible with EVA, PVC and polyolefin containers. Do not co-administer other medicinal products through the same infusion line. Flush line at end of infusion with sodium chloride 0.9% or glucose 5%. Treatment duration: up to 2 years.

dose_modifications.notes: Discuss with appropriate clinician if criteria not met. Proceed if neutrophils ≥ 1.0 x10⁹/L.
Discuss with appropriate clinician if criteria not met. Proceed if platelets ≥ 75 x10⁹/L.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT > 3 and ≤ 5 times ULN, or total bilirubin > 1.5 and ≤ 3 times ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment. Initiate corticosteroids 0.5–1 mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents. May resume after corticosteroid taper if improvement occurs. If no improvement, escalate corticosteroids to 1–2 mg/kg/day and permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: AST or ALT > 5 times ULN regardless of baseline, or total bilirubin > 3 times ULN, or concurrent AST/ALT > 3 times ULN and total bilirubin > 2 times ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue. Initiate corticosteroids 1–2 mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents.
Withhold and initiate corticosteroids 0.5–1 mg/kg/day. May resume after taper on improvement. If no improvement escalate to 1–2 mg/kg/day and permanently discontinue.
Permanently discontinue. Initiate corticosteroids 1–2 mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents.
Withhold dose(s) until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, and corticosteroid management is complete.
Permanently discontinue treatment.
Withhold dose(s) until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management is complete.
Permanently discontinue treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Symptomatic Grade 2 or 3 hypothyroidism/hyperthyroidism/hypophysitis, Grade 2 adrenal insufficiency, Grade 3 diabetes
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until symptoms resolve. Treatment may continue in presence of hormone replacement therapy as long as no symptoms are present.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 hypothyroidism/hyperthyroidism/hypophysitis, Grade 3 or 4 adrenal insufficiency, Grade 4 diabetes
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue treatment.
Withhold dose(s). Grade 3 rash: withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete. Suspected SJS/TEN: withhold dose(s).
Permanently discontinue treatment.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete.
Permanently discontinue treatment.
Withhold dose(s).

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 or recurrent Grade 3; persistent Grade 2 or 3 despite treatment modification; inability to reduce corticosteroid to ≤10mg prednisone/day
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue treatment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Proceed with treatment if neutrophils ≥ 1.0 x10⁹/L and platelets ≥ 75 x10⁹/L. Discuss with clinician if criteria not met.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then every 6 weeks or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose escalation or reduction is NOT recommended. Doses are delayed, not reduced, for toxicity.
Opdualag should not be resumed while the patient is receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy.
For suspected immune-related adverse reactions, corticosteroid taper should be of at least 1 month duration. Rapid tapering may lead to worsening or recurrence.
Non-corticosteroid immunosuppressive therapy should be added if there is worsening or no improvement despite corticosteroid use.
Prophylactic antibiotics may be used in patients receiving immunosuppressive therapy.

required_patient_fields.ecog_ps: Required: ECOG PS 0 or 1 (or Lansky ≥80% if aged 12-17)
required_patient_fields.prior_checkpoint_inhibitor: Must not have received prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1/PD-L2/anti-CTLA-4 for unresectable/metastatic melanoma

drugs.special_instructions: Nivolumab 480mg combined with Relatlimab 160mg as fixed-dose combination (Opdualag). Flat dose — doses are delayed, not reduced, for toxicity. Use in-line or add-on sterile, non-pyrogenic, low protein binding filter (pore size 0.2–1.2 µm). Compatible with EVA, PVC and polyolefin containers. Do not co-administer other medicinal products through the same infusion line. Flush line at end of infusion with sodium chloride 0.9% or glucose 5%. Treatment duration: up to 2 years.
drugs.notes: Every 28 days | Nivolumab 480mg combined with Relatlimab 160mg as fixed-dose combination (Opdualag). Flat dose — doses are delayed, not reduced, for toxicity. Use in-line or add-on sterile, non-pyrogenic, low protein binding filter (pore size 0.2–1.2 µm). Compatible with EVA, PVC and polyolefin containers. Do not co-administer other medicinal products through the same infusion line. Flush line at end of infusion with sodium chloride 0.9% or glucose 5%. Treatment duration: up to 2 years.

indication: The treatment of advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma in adults. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands. Administer via 0.2–1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Polyethylene lined giving sets used for paclitaxel with 0.22 micron filter are appropriate. Extravasation risk: neutral.

dose_modifications.condition: Little need to adjust for haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL.

Withhold and administer corticosteroids. On improvement to grade 1 taper corticosteroid over 1 month. May restart when LFTs remain grade 1 after taper. Permanently discontinue if: no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks; corticosteroids cannot be reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone/day within 12 weeks; or any grade 3+ reaction.
Permanently discontinue in first instance.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 2 or 3 serum creatinine elevation
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and initiate corticosteroids. Taper over at least 1 month on improvement to grade 1. May resume when reaction remains grade 1 or below following taper. Permanently discontinue if: no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks; corticosteroids cannot be reduced to ≤10mg/day within 12 weeks; or recurrent grade 3.
Permanently discontinue. Initiate corticosteroids 1–2 mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents.
Withhold until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, and corticosteroid management is complete.
Permanently discontinue.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete.
Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until laboratory values return to baseline and corticosteroid management complete.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold until creatinine returns to baseline and corticosteroid management complete.
Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Symptomatic endocrinopathies (hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, hypophysitis, adrenal insufficiency, diabetes)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until symptoms resolve. Continue in presence of hormone replacement therapy if no symptoms. Isolated hypothyroidism may be managed with replacement therapy without interruption.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management complete.
Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 immune-related adverse reactions that persist despite treatment modifications or corticosteroid dose cannot be reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone equivalent
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Little need to adjust dose for haematological toxicity. Dose escalation or reduction not recommended.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then every 6 weeks or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose escalation or reduction is NOT recommended for nivolumab — delay or discontinue only.
Do not resume nivolumab if the patient is still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy.
Systemic corticosteroids before starting nivolumab should be avoided due to potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity.
Patients must be given a nivolumab Patient Alert Card.
Immune-related adverse reactions may onset months after the last dose — early diagnosis and management essential.

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands. Administer via 0.2–1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Polyethylene lined giving sets used for paclitaxel with 0.22 micron filter are appropriate. Extravasation risk: neutral.
drugs.notes: Every 14 days | Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands. Administer via 0.2–1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Polyethylene lined giving sets used for paclitaxel with 0.22 micron filter are appropriate. Extravasation risk: neutral.

indication: Pembrolizumab is recommended: as an option for treating adults with advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma that has not been previously treated with ipilimumab; as an option for treating advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma in adults only after the disease has progressed with ipilimumab and, for BRAF V600 mutation-positive disease, a BRAF or MEK inhibitor. WHO Performance Status 0 or 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer using a low protein binding filter (0.2 micron). Extravasation risk: neutral. Treat until disease progression/loss of clinical benefit, excessive toxicity, or patient choice (35 cycles or two years set in ARIA). Funding precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

dose_modifications.condition: Little need to adjust for haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL. No routine dose adjustment for haematological toxicity.

Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Taper over 1 month on improvement to grade 1. May restart when LFTs remain grade 1 after taper. Permanently discontinue if no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks, corticosteroids cannot be reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone/day within 12 weeks, or any grade 3+ reaction.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT ≥ 5xULN; or AST/ALT increase ≥50% from baseline lasting ≥1 week in patients with liver metastasis starting with grade 2 elevation; or bilirubin > 3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue in first instance.

Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Taper over at least 1 month on improvement to grade 1 or below. Permanently discontinue if no improvement to grade 1 within 12 weeks, corticosteroids cannot be reduced to ≤10mg/day within 12 weeks, or any grade 3+ reaction.
Permanently discontinue.

Withhold and initiate corticosteroids 1–2 mg/kg/day prednisone or equivalent. Taper over ≥4 weeks on improvement to grade 1. May resume if event improves to grade 0/1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone/day. Discontinue if second episode.
Permanently discontinue. Consider IV methylprednisolone 125mg, then oral prednisone 1–2 mg/kg/day. Consider infliximab 5mg/kg if no improvement within 48–72 hours.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Taper over 1 month on improvement to grade 1.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3+ colitis not improving, or corticosteroids cannot be tapered
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold and consider dermatology referral. Systemic corticosteroids (prednisolone 1mg/kg/day). Taper over ≥4 weeks on improvement to grade 1.

dose_modifications.condition: Any NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis, nephritis, infusion related reaction, or grade 4 adverse reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds stated — little need to adjust for haematological toxicity.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab — delay or discontinue only.
Systemic corticosteroids before starting pembrolizumab should be avoided due to potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity.
Current funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer.
Immune-related adverse reactions may onset months after the last dose — early diagnosis essential.
Do not resume pembrolizumab while receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids.

indication: The combination of dabrafenib and trametinib is recommended as an option for the first line treatment of stage III (unresectable) or stage IV BRAF V600 mutation positive malignant melanoma. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Swallow whole with water on an empty stomach, either 1 hour before or 2 hours after a meal, approximately 12 hours apart. Do not crush or chew. Available as 50mg and 75mg capsules. If a dose is missed, do not take if less than 6 hours until the next dose. Dose may be modified in 50mg or 75mg steps. Not recommended in patients with uncorrectable electrolyte abnormalities (including magnesium), long QT syndrome (QTc >500ms), or taking medicines known to prolong QT interval. Follow NPSA/2008/RRR001 alert.

Take at least 1 hour before or 2 hours after a meal. Available as 0.5mg and 2mg tablets. If a dose is missed, only take if more than 12 hours until next scheduled dose. Dose should not be reduced below 1mg once daily. Follow NPSA/2008/RRR001 alert.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Take on empty stomach 1 hour before or 2 hours after a meal, approximately 12 hours apart. Do not crush or chew.
Take at least 1 hour before or 2 hours after a meal.

dose_modifications.notes: Prior to prescribing: neutrophils must be ≥ 1.5 x10⁹/L.
Prior to prescribing: platelets must be ≥ 100 x10⁹/L.

dose_modifications.parameter: Dabrafenib toxicity — Grade 1 or tolerable Grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Continue at current dose and monitor.

dose_modifications.parameter: Dabrafenib toxicity — intolerable Grade 2 or Grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until resolved to grade 0–1. First occurrence: reduce to 100mg twice daily. Second occurrence: reduce to 75mg twice daily. Third occurrence: reduce to 50mg twice daily.
Permanently discontinue, OR interrupt until resolved to grade 0–1 then reduce to 100mg twice daily (discuss with consultant). If grade 4 recurs, permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.parameter: Trametinib toxicity — Grade 1 or tolerable Grade 2
dose_modifications.notes: Continue and monitor.

dose_modifications.parameter: Trametinib toxicity — intolerable Grade 2 or Grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until toxicity is grade 0–1 and reduce by one dose level when resuming. Dose ladder: 2mg → 1.5mg → 1mg once daily (minimum 1mg/day).
Permanently discontinue, OR interrupt until grade 0–1 and reduce by one dose level when resuming.
Treatment not recommended.
Interrupt until QTc < 500ms. Correct electrolyte imbalances (including Mg). Assess cardiac risk factors. First occurrence: reduce to 100mg BD. Second: 75mg BD. Third: 50mg BD.
Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Asymptomatic absolute decrease in LVEF ≥10% from baseline and below institutional LLN
dose_modifications.notes: Do not modify dabrafenib. Withhold trametinib for up to 4 weeks. If LVEF improves to normal, resume at lower dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue trametinib.
Permanently discontinue trametinib. No modification of dabrafenib required.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI CTC grade 2–3 retinal pigment epithelial detachment
dose_modifications.notes: Do not modify dabrafenib. Withhold trametinib for up to 3 weeks. If improved to grade 0–1, resume at lower dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue.
Withhold dabrafenib for up to 6 weeks. If improved to grade 0–1, resume at same dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue. Do not modify trametinib.

dose_modifications.condition: Suspected or confirmed ILD or pneumonitis
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold trametinib while under investigation. Permanently discontinue for treatment-related ILD/pneumonitis. No modification of dabrafenib required.

Administer antipyretics (paracetamol/ibuprofen). Interrupt dabrafenib but continue trametinib. Restart dabrafenib at same dose when pyrexia resolves. If pyrexia associated with dehydration/hypotension/renal insufficiency, reduce dabrafenib by one dose level and give oral corticosteroids (prednisone 10mg) for ≥5 days.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Neutrophils ≥ 1.5 x10⁹/L and platelets ≥ 100 x10⁹/L required prior to prescribing.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es including magnesium prior to day one of each cycle
Baseline chest CT then 6-monthly
Baseline ECG, repeated after 1 month and then as required (after dose changes). Monthly for 3 months, then 3-monthly in patients with moderate to severe hepatic impairment
BRAF V600 mutation status prior to starting therapy
LVEF assessment (echocardiogram or MUGA) for trametinib — baseline and as required
special_warnings: Dabrafenib is NOT recommended in patients with uncorrectable electrolyte abnormalities (including magnesium), long QT syndrome (QTc > 500ms), or taking medicines known to prolong the QT interval.
Trametinib minimum dose is 1mg once daily — dose reductions below this are not recommended.
Third dose reduction requires treatment cessation.
Dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 alert must be followed for both dabrafenib and trametinib.
Risk of secondary cutaneous or non-cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma with dabrafenib.
Extended ECG monitoring required in patients with moderate/severe hepatic impairment.

required_patient_fields.braf_mutation: Required: BRAF V600 mutation positive status confirmed

indication: Dabrafenib is recommended as an option for the first line treatment of unresectable BRAF V600 mutation positive melanoma. WHO performance status 0, 1.

indication: Dacarbazine is recommended as a first line option for unresectable stage IIIC or IV melanoma in those unsuitable for ipilimumab. Dacarbazine is recommended as an option for the second line treatment of unresectable stage IIIC or IV melanoma of cutaneous, ocular or mucosal origin following treatment with ipilimumab. Dacarbazine is recommended as an option for the third line treatment of unresectable stage IIIC or IV melanoma of cutaneous, ocular or mucosal origin. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Dacarbazine is a vesicant — extravasation precautions required. Light sensitive — protect infusion from light during administration. Do not use if solution has pink or red discolouration.

pre_medications.notes: May be given orally or intravenously
May be given orally or intravenously

take_home_medicines.frequency: Twice daily for 3 days starting the day after chemotherapy
take_home_medicines.instructions: Take twice daily for 3 days starting the day after chemotherapy

take_home_medicines.frequency: Three times a day for 3 days starting on the day of chemotherapy, then when required
take_home_medicines.instructions: Take three times a day for 3 days starting on the day of chemotherapy, then 10mg three times a day when required

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. Repeat FBC. If counts return to eligible levels, continue at 100% of last dose.
Delay treatment for 7 days. Repeat FBC. If counts return to eligible levels, continue at 100% of last dose.
Dacarbazine is activated and metabolised in the liver and can be hepatotoxic. Consider dose reduction or stopping therapy if baseline LFTs double.
Reduce to 80% of original dose.
Reduce to 75% of original dose.
Reduce to 70% of original dose.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: If platelets < 100 x10⁹/L and neutrophils < 1.5 x10⁹/L, delay treatment for 7 days and repeat FBC.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Renal function (creatinine clearance) for dose adjustment
special_warnings: Dacarbazine is a VESICANT — extravasation precautions required.
Dacarbazine is light sensitive — protect infusion from light during administration.
Do not use if solution has pink or red discolouration.
Influenza-type syndrome may start up to 7 days after treatment and last up to 21 days.
Risk of anaphylaxis — appropriate management should be available.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Dacarbazine is a vesicant — extravasation precautions required. Light sensitive — protect infusion from light during administration. Do not use if solution has pink or red discolouration.
drugs.notes: Every 21 days | Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Dacarbazine is a vesicant — extravasation precautions required. Light sensitive — protect infusion from light during administration. Do not use if solution has pink or red discolouration.

indication: Ipilimumab is recommended, within its marketing authorisation, as an option for treating adults with previously untreated advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma, only if the manufacturer provides ipilimumab with the discount agreed in the patient access scheme. Ipilimumab is recommended as an option for treating advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma in people who have received prior therapy, only if the manufacturer provides ipilimumab with the discount agreed in the patient access scheme. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1–4mg/ml. Extravasation risk: neutral. Doses omitted due to adverse reactions must NOT be replaced.

dose_modifications.condition: AST or ALT 5–8xULN or total bilirubin 3–5xULN (suspected immune-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold scheduled dose until LFTs resolve to these levels, then restart.

dose_modifications.condition: AST or ALT > 8xULN or bilirubin > 5xULN (suspected immune-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue and initiate appropriate supportive treatment.
Little need to adjust ipilimumab dose for haematological toxicity. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if Hb < 8g/dL.
No dose reduction necessary in mild to moderate renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 1 or 2 diarrhoea or mild to moderate colitis
dose_modifications.notes: May continue on ipilimumab with symptomatic treatment and monitoring. If symptoms recur or persist for 5–7 days, omit scheduled dose. Consider prednisolone 1mg/kg orally once daily. If resolution to grade 0–1, resume at next scheduled dose.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or 4 diarrhoea, colitis, peritoneal signs, ileus, or fever
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue. Initiate high-dose IV corticosteroids immediately (unless bowel perforation). Taper over 6–8 weeks. Consider infliximab 5mg/kg (single dose) if no response to steroids within 5–7 days.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 2 neuropathy likely related to ipilimumab
dose_modifications.notes: Omit scheduled dose. If neurologic symptoms resolve to baseline, may resume at next scheduled dose.
Permanently discontinue. Treat according to local guidelines for sensory neuropathy.
Omit scheduled dose. If symptoms improve to grade 1 or resolve, may resume at next scheduled dose.

dose_modifications.parameter: Immune-related skin — Grade 4 or Grade 3 pruritus
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 4 rash or Grade 3 pruritus
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue. Consider systemic corticosteroid therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Other immune-related adverse reactions at NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Consider immediate high-dose corticosteroid therapy and discontinuation of ipilimumab.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds for ipilimumab. Little need to adjust for haematological toxicity. Blood pressure should be checked prior to treatment.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Blood pressure prior to treatment
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for ipilimumab (immunotherapy) — doses are delayed or permanently discontinued only.
Doses omitted due to adverse reactions must NOT be replaced.
Systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab should be avoided due to potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity.
Immune-related adverse reactions may involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine, or other organ systems — may onset months after last dose.
Ipilimumab is used at 3mg/kg in this skin protocol (not 10mg/kg — confirm indication carefully).
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.prior_therapy: Document whether previously treated or treatment-naive

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1–4mg/ml. Extravasation risk: neutral. Doses omitted due to adverse reactions must NOT be replaced.
drugs.notes: Every 21 days for 4 cycles | Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1–4mg/ml. Extravasation risk: neutral. Doses omitted due to adverse reactions must NOT be replaced.
